title: Enkleia andamanica
taxonomy: Enkleia andamanica is classified in the family Annonaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family comprises over 2,000 species, with many of them being tropical trees or shrubs. Enkleia andamanica is a species of flowering plant endemic to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, a union territory of India. Its classification is further narrowed down to the genus Enkleia, which is a relatively small group of plants within the Annonaceae family. The combination of its unique characteristics and classification makes Enkleia andamanica an interesting subject for botanical study.
